volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Braggart Soldier (Section 2.2.236b - Section 2.3.310)

translation.label: Scaife English translation
translation.citation: Miles Gloriosus, or The Braggart Captain

sections.label: Section 2.2.236b
sections.translation: I myself know the same thing .

sections.label: Section 2.2.237
sections.original: Nunc sic rationem incipisso , hánc instituam astutiam ,
sections.translation: Now , thus I would begin upon my plan ; this contrivance I shall act upon . I shall say that her other own twin-sister has come here from Athens , with a certain person , her lover , to Philocomasium ,

sections.label: Section 2.2.240
sections.original: tam similem , quam lacte lactist ; ápud te eos hic devortier
sections.translation: as like to her as milk is to milk . I shall say that they are lodged and entertained here in your house .

sections.label: Section 2.2.241b
sections.original: Euge euge , lepide , laudo commentum tuom .
sections.translation: Bravo ! bravo ! cleverly thought of . I approve or your device .

sections.label: Section 2.2.242
sections.original: Vt si illic concriminatus sit advorsum militem
sections.translation: So that , if my fellow-servant should accuse her before the Captain , and say that he has seen her here at your house , toying with another man , I shall assert , on the other hand , that my fellow-servant has seen the other one , the sister , at your house ,

sections.label: Section 2.2.245
sections.original: cum suo amatore amplexantem atque osculantem .
sections.translation: fondling and toying with her own lover .

sections.label: Section 2.2.245b
sections.translation: Aye , most excellent . I ’ ll say the same , if the Captain shall inquire of me .

sections.label: Section 2.2.246b
sections.translation: But do you say that they are extremely alike ; and this must be imparted in time to Philocomasium , in order that she may know ; that she mayn ’ t be tripping if the Captain should question her .

sections.label: Section 2.2.248b
sections.translation: A very clever contrivance . But if the Captain should wish to see them both in company together ,

sections.label: Section 2.2.250
sections.translation: what shall we do then ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.250b
sections.translation: That ’ s easy enough . Three hundred excuses may be picked up — she is not at home ; she has gone out walking ; she is asleep ; she is dressing ; she is bathing ; she is at breakfast [*] ; she is taking dessert [*] ; she is engaged ; she is enjoying her rest [*] ; in fact , she can ’ t come . There are as many of these put-offs as you like , if I can only persuade him at the very outset to believe that to be true which shall be contrived .

sections.label: Section 2.2.255
sections.translation: I like what you say .

sections.label: Section 2.2.255b
sections.original: Intro abi ergo , et si isti est mulier , eam iube
sections.translation: Go in-doors then ; and if the damsel ’ s there , bid her return home directly , and instruct and tutor her thoroughly in this plan , that she may understand our scheme , as we have begun it , about the twin-sister .

sections.label: Section 2.2.258b
sections.translation: I ’ ll have her right cleverly tutor ’ d for you . Is there anything else ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.259b
sections.translation: Only , be off in-doors .

sections.label: Section 2.2.259c
sections.translation: I ’ m off .

sections.label: Section 2.2.259d
sections.translation: And I ’ ll go home , too ;

sections.label: Section 2.2.260
sections.original: atque hominem investigando operam huic dissimulabiliter dabo ,
sections.translation: and I ’ ll conceal the fact that I am giving her my aid in seeking out the man , which fellow-servant of mine it was , that to-day was following the monkey . For it cannot be but in his conversation he must have made some one of the household acquainted about the lady of his master , how that he himself has seen her next door here toying with some stranger spark .

sections.label: Section 2.2.265
sections.original: novi morem : egomet tacere nequeo solus quod scio .
sections.translation: I know the habit myself ; I can ’ t hold my tongue on that which I know alone . If I find out the person who saw it , I ’ ll plant against him all my mantelets [*] and covered works . The material is prepared ; ’ tis a sure matter that I must take this person by force , and by thus besieging him . If so I don ’ t find the man , just like a hound I ’ ll go smelling about , even until I shall have traced out the fox by his track .

sections.label: Section 2.2.270
sections.original: sed fores crepuerunt nostrae , ego voci moderabor meae ;
sections.translation: But our door makes a noise : I ’ ll lower my voice ; for here is the keeper of Philocomasium , my fellow-servant , coming out of doors .

sections.label: Section 2.3.272
sections.original: Nisi quidem ego hodie ambulavi dormiens in tegulis ,
sections.translation: Unless , in fact , I have been walking this day in my sleep upon the tiles , i ’ faith , I know for sure that I have seen here , at our neighbour ’ s next door , Philocomasium , the lady of my master , on the high road [*] to mischief to herself .

sections.label: Section 2.3.275
sections.original: Hic illam vidit osculantem , quantum hunc audivi loqui .
sections.translation: ’ Twas he that saw her billing , so far as I have heard him say .

sections.label: Section 2.3.276
sections.translation: Who ’ s that ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.276b
sections.translation: Your fellow-servant . How are you , Sceledrus ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.276c
sections.translation: I am glad that I have met you , Palaestrio .

sections.label: Section 2.3.277b
sections.original: Quid iam ? aut quid negotist ? fac sciam .
sections.translation: What now ? Or what ’ s the matter ? Let me know .

sections.label: Section 2.3.278
sections.translation: I ’ m afraid .

sections.label: Section 2.3.278b
sections.translation: What are you afraid of ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.278c
sections.original: Ne hercle hodie , quantum hic familiariumst ,
sections.translation: By my troth , lest , this day , as many domestics as there are of us here , we shall jump into a most woful punishmient by way of torture .

sections.label: Section 2.3.279b
sections.translation: Jump you alone , please ;

sections.label: Section 2.3.280
sections.original: solus , nám ego istam insulturam et desulturam nil moror .
sections.translation: for I don ’ t at all like this jumping in [*] and jumping out .

sections.label: Section 2.3.281
sections.original: Nescis tu fortasse , apud nos facinus quod natumst novom .
sections.translation: Perhaps you don ’ t know what new mischance has happened at home ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.282
sections.translation: What mischance is this ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.282b
sections.translation: A disgraceful one .

sections.label: Section 2.3.282c
sections.translation: Do you then keep it to yourself alone : don ’ t tell it me ; I don ’ t want to know it .

sections.label: Section 2.3.283b
sections.translation: But I won ’ t let you not know it . To-day I was following our monkey upon the tiles , next door there .

sections.label: Section 2.3.285
sections.original: Edepol , Sceledre , homo sectatus essectatu's nihili nequam bestiam .
sections.translation: By my troth , Sceledrus , a worthless fellow , you were following a worthless beast .

sections.label: Section 2.3.286
sections.translation: The Gods confound you !

sections.label: Section 2.3.286b
sections.original: Te istuc aequom — quoniam occepisti , eloqui .
sections.translation: That befits yourself , since you began the conversation .

sections.label: Section 2.3.287
sections.original: Forte fortuna per impluvium huc despexi in proxumum ,
sections.translation: By chance , as it happened , I looked down there through the skylight , into the next house ; and there I saw Philocomasium toying with some strange young man , I know not whom .

sections.label: Section 2.3.289b
sections.original: Quod ego , Sceledre , scelus ex te audio ?
sections.translation: What scandalous thing is this I hear of you , Sceledrus ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.290
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I did see her , beyond a doubt .

sections.label: Section 2.3.290b
sections.translation: What , yourself ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.290c
sections.translation: Yes , I myself , with these eyes of mine .

sections.label: Section 2.3.291
sections.original: Abi , non verisimile dicis , neque vidisti .
sections.translation: Get away , it isn ’ t likely what you say , nor did you see her .

sections.label: Section 2.3.291b
sections.translation: Do I , then , appear to you as if I were purblind ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.292b
sections.translation: ’ Twere better for you to ask the doctor about that . But , indeed , if the Gods only love you , don ’ t you rashly father this [*] idle story . Now are you breeding thence a fatal dilemma for your legs and head ;

sections.label: Section 2.3.295
sections.original: nam tibi iam ut pereas paratum est dupliciter , nisi supprimis
sections.translation: for , in two ways , the cause is contrived for you to be ruined , unless you put a check upon your foolish chattering .

sections.label: Section 2.3.296b
sections.translation: But how , two ways ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.296c
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell you . First then , if you falsely accuse Philocomasium , by that you are undone ; in the next place , if it is true , having been appointed her keeper , there you are undone .

sections.label: Section 2.3.299
sections.original: Quid fuat me , nescio : haec me vidisse ego certo scio .
sections.translation: What may happen to me , I know not ; I know for certain that I did see this .

sections.label: Section 2.3.300
sections.translation: Do you persist in it , unfortunate wretch ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.300b
sections.original: Quid tibi vis dicam nisi quod viderim ?
sections.translation: What would you have me say to you , but that I did see her ? Moreover , she is in there , next door , at this very moment .

sections.label: Section 2.3.301b
sections.translation: What ! Isn ’ t she at home ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.302
sections.original: Vise , abi intro tute , nam ego mi iam nil credi postulo .
sections.translation: Go and see . Go in-doors yourself ; for I don ’ t ask now for any confidence to be put in me .

sections.label: Section 2.3.303
sections.translation: I ’ m determined to do so .

sections.label: Section 2.3.303b
sections.original: Hic te opperiar ; eadem illi insidias dabo ,
sections.translation: I ’ ll wait here for you .

sections.label: Section 2.3.304
sections.original: quam mox horsum ad stabulum iuuenix recipiat se a pabulo .
sections.translation: In this direction will I be on the watch for her , [*] how soon the heifer may betake herself from the pasture this way towards her stall .

sections.label: Section 2.3.305
sections.original: quid ego nunc faciam ? custodem me illi miles addidit :
sections.translation: What now shall I do ? The Captain gave me to her as her keeper . Now , if I make a discovery , I ’ m undone ; if I am silent , still I am undone , if this should be discovered . What is there more abandoned or more daring than a woman ? While I was upon the tiles , this woman betook herself out of doors from her dwelling . By my troth , ’ twas a brazen act she did . If , now , the Captain were to know of this ,

sections.label: Section 2.3.310
sections.original: credo hercle has sustollat aedis totas atque hunc in crucem .
sections.translation: i ’ faith , I believe he would pull down the whole entire house next door , and me he would send to the gibbet . [*] Whatever comes of it , i ’ faith , I ’ ll hold my tongue rather than come to a bad end . I cannot keep effectual guard on a woman that puts herself up for sale .
